ru.title: Ускорение видеогенерации через выборочный денойзинг ключевых кадров
ru.desc: RhymeFlow — это метод ускорения диффузионных трансформеров для генерации видео, который разделяет процесс денойзинга для разных кадров видеопоследовательности. Вместо того чтобы обрабатывать каждый кадр полностью через все шаги диффузии, метод выделяет ключевые кадры с критическими семантическими переходами и подвергает только их полному денойзингу. Промежуточные кадры пропускают некоторые шаги денойзинга, сокращая вычислительные затраты, однако модуль проекции латентных траекторий обеспечивает взаимодействие между ключевыми кадрами и полной темпорально согласованной последовательностью. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что RhymeFlow достигает более высокой скорости инференса при сохранении или улучшении качества генерируемых видео.

en.title: Accelerating Video Generation with Smart Denoising
en.desc: RhymeFlow is a novel framework designed to enhance the efficiency of video generation using diffusion transformers by optimizing the denoising process. It identifies keyframes that capture essential changes in the video and applies detailed denoising only to these frames, while allowing non-keyframes to skip some denoising steps. This approach reduces computational costs without sacrificing visual quality, as it maintains coherence through a latent trajectory projection module. Extensive testing shows that RhymeFlow significantly improves inference speed and visual fidelity compared to traditional methods.

zh.title: RhymeFlow：高效视频生成的新方法
zh.desc: RhymeFlow 是一种加速视频生成的扩散变换器框架，通过解耦不同帧的去噪轨迹来提高效率。它通过锚定关键帧和潜在轨迹投影，确保视觉质量的同时减少计算开销。该方法只对关键帧进行密集的逐步去噪，而非关键帧则逐步跳过去噪步骤，从而降低计算成本。实验结果表明，RhymeFlow 在推理速度和视觉质量上均优于现有的基线方法。

papers.title: ClinHallu: A Benchmark for Diagnosing Stage-Wise Hallucinations in Medical MLLM Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract ClinHallu is a benchmark for diagnosing and mitigating hallucinations in medical multimodal large language models through stage-wise reasoning analysis and trace-supervised fine-tuning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Building trustworthy medical multimodal large language models (MLLMs) is critical for reliable clinical decision support. Existing medical hallucination benchmarks mainly focus on data collection, but often ignore where hallucinations originate within the reasoning process. We find that hallucination sources vary across samples: errors may arise from visual misrecognition, incorrect medical knowledge recall, or flawed reasoning integration. To enable source-level hallucination diagnosis, we introduce ClinHallu, a benchmark for stage-wise hallucination diagnosis in medical MLLM reasoning. ClinHallu contains 7,031 validated instances, where each instance is augmented with a structured reasoning trace decomposed into Visual Recognition, Knowledge Recall, and Reasoning Integration. We also use stage-replacement interventions to measure how correcting specific stages affects the final answer. Beyond evaluation, we show that trace-supervised fine-tuning reduces stage-wise hallucinations. ClinHallu provides a fine-grained hallucination testbed for diagnosing and mitigating reasoning failures in medical MLLMs. The benchmark is publicly available at https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/ClinHallu.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)

ru.title: Диагностика и устранение галлюцинаций в медицинских мультимодальных моделях через анализ этапов рассуждения
ru.desc: ClinHallu — это эталонный набор для диагностики галлюцинаций в медицинских мультимодальных большие языковых моделях, которые анализируют изображения и текст одновременно. Исследователи выявили, что ошибки возникают на разных этапах рассуждения: при распознавании визуального контента, воспроизведении медицинских знаний или интеграции полученной информации. Каждый пример в наборе данных из 7031 экземпляра содержит структурированную трассу рассуждения, что позволяет точно определить источник ошибки. Авторы также показали, что обучение с использованием разметки этапов рассуждения эффективно снижает галлюцинации на каждом уровне.

en.title: Diagnosing and Mitigating Hallucinations in Medical MLLMs with ClinHallu
en.desc: ClinHallu is a new benchmark designed to identify and reduce hallucinations in medical multimodal large language models (MLLMs) by analyzing their reasoning processes. It focuses on diagnosing where hallucinations occur, such as in visual recognition, knowledge recall, or reasoning integration. The benchmark includes over 7,000 validated instances, each with a detailed reasoning trace to help pinpoint errors. Additionally, it demonstrates that fine-tuning the model using these traces can effectively decrease hallucinations during reasoning.

zh.title: ClinHallu：医疗推理中的幻觉诊断与减轻
zh.desc: ClinHallu是一个用于诊断和减轻医疗多模态大语言模型（MLLM）幻觉的基准，通过阶段性推理分析和追踪监督微调来实现。现有的医疗幻觉基准主要关注数据收集，但往往忽视了幻觉在推理过程中的来源。我们发现幻觉来源因样本而异：错误可能源于视觉误识别、医学知识回忆不准确或推理整合缺陷。ClinHallu提供了一个细粒度的幻觉测试平台，用于诊断和减轻医疗MLLM中的推理失败。

papers.title: LoSoNA: A Benchmark for Local Social Norm Adaptation in Group Conversations
papers.abstract: Abstract LoSoNA evaluates how well large language models can infer and adapt to implicit local conversational norms in group chat settings through targeted prompting scenarios.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Online group chats are social spaces with local conversational norms that are rarely stated explicitly. The ability and willingness of LLM-based agents to recognize and adapt to these norms remains mostly unexplored. We introduce LoSoNA, a benchmark for local social norm adaptation in multi-party chat. Each scenario gives a subject model a curated group-chat transcript in which non-subject participants demonstrate a hidden local norm, followed by a final elicitor turn that forces a response revealing whether the subject has inferred that norm. We evaluate eight frontier and open-weight models under four prompting conditions that vary how explicitly the model is told to treat the prior conversation as evidence for how it should answer. Naive prompting remains limited for most models; explicit norm-aware prompting helps unevenly, with Gemini 3.1 Pro reaching 84.2% and Claude Fable 5 reaching 81.6%, while several other models show small gains or regressions. LoSoNA contributes to recent calls for evaluating LLM social capabilities by testing whether models can infer local conversational norms from precedent and use them in a one-turn group-chat response.

ru.title: Распознавание скрытых социальных норм в групповых диалогах
ru.desc: В этой работе представлен LoSoNA — бенчмарк для оценки способности больших языковых моделей распознавать и адаптироваться к неявным социальным нормам в групповых чатах. Исследователи создали сценарии, где модель должна вывести скрытую норму из предыдущего диалога и продемонстрировать её понимание в своем ответе. Были протестированы восемь передовых моделей при четырёх условиях промптирования, варьирующих от обычного до явного указания следовать выявленным нормам. Результаты показали, что явное указание на норму-ориентированное промптирование помогает неравномерно: Gemini 3.1 Pro достигла 84.2% точности, Claude Fable 5 — 81.6%, в то время как другие модели показали незначительные улучшения или снижение производительности.

en.title: Evaluating Language Models on Local Conversational Norms
en.desc: LoSoNA is a benchmark designed to assess how well large language models (LLMs) can understand and adapt to implicit social norms in group chat environments. It involves presenting models with chat transcripts where participants exhibit hidden norms, followed by a prompt that tests the model's ability to infer these norms. The study evaluates various LLMs under different prompting strategies, revealing that while naive prompting is often ineffective, explicit norm-aware prompting significantly improves performance for some models. The findings highlight the importance of evaluating LLMs' social capabilities in recognizing and responding to local conversational dynamics.

zh.title: 评估大型语言模型的社交规范适应能力
zh.desc: LoSoNA是一个评估大型语言模型在群聊中推断和适应隐含社交规范能力的基准。该研究通过特定的提示场景，考察模型如何从群聊记录中识别和适应这些隐含的社交规范。实验结果显示，尽管大多数模型在简单提示下表现有限，但明确的规范意识提示能显著提高模型的表现。特别是，Gemini 3.1 Pro和Claude Fable 5在适应社交规范方面取得了较高的准确率。

papers.title: When is Your LLM Steerable?
papers.abstract: Abstract Activation steering effectiveness can be predicted from early decoding states using a GBDT classifier, enabling efficient steering strength optimization with reduced computational cost.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Activation steering offers a lightweight approach to control language models' behavior at inference time, but whether it succeeds or fails heavily depends on the prompt, concept, model, and steering configuration. Finding the regime and boundaries of successful steering typically requires expensive grid searches and post-hoc evaluation of full autoregressive rollouts. In this work, we investigate whether steerability can be predicted from the model's internal states at the beginning of the generation process, e.g., after generating the first few tokens, and how to leverage such a predictor to improve steering success rate. To this end, we first introduce ASTEER, a testbed including 1.4M steered generations, spanning 150 concepts with each steering success/failure labeled. Leveraging this testbed, we analyze the model's early decoding dynamics by extracting features that compare hidden states before and after steering across layers and initial decoding steps. These features help us understand how steering's effects propagate along layers and token positions, which provide key information for steerability prediction. We then train a Gradient Boosting Decision Trees (GBDT) classifier on these features to predict whether an intervention will under-steer, succeed, or over-steer without requiring full rollout. Our predictor achieves around 0.7 macro-F1 score on unseen concepts, demonstrating that early hidden states encode substantial, structured information about eventual steering efficacy. We further leverage this steerability predictor as guidance for steering strength searching, achieving near-optimal performance with a small fraction of decoding cost.

ru.title: Предсказание успеха управления активациями по начальным состояниям модели
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуется, можно ли предсказать эффективность стирования активаций (activation steering) в языковых моделях на ранних стадиях генерации текста. Авторы создали тестбенч ASTEER с 1,4 миллионами примеров управления моделью и выделили признаки из скрытых состояний модели, которые показывают, как эффект стирования распространяется по слоям и позициям токенов. Они обучили классификатор на основе градиентного бустинга (GBDT), который может предсказать результат стирования (недостаточное, успешное или чрезмерное) без полной генерации текста. Полученный предсказатель достигает F1-оценки около 0,7 и позволяет оптимизировать силу стирования с минимальными вычислительными затратами.

en.title: Predicting Steering Success with Early Decoding Insights
en.desc: This paper explores how to predict the effectiveness of activation steering in language models using early decoding states. By employing a Gradient Boosting Decision Trees (GBDT) classifier, the authors demonstrate that it is possible to optimize steering strength efficiently without extensive computational resources. They introduce a testbed called ASTEER, which includes a large dataset of steered generations, allowing for the analysis of the model's internal states during the initial decoding process. The findings indicate that early hidden states contain valuable information that can significantly enhance the success rate of steering interventions.

zh.title: 通过早期状态预测激活引导的成功率
zh.desc: 本文研究了如何通过模型生成过程初期的内部状态来预测激活引导的有效性。我们提出了ASTEER测试平台，包含140万次引导生成的实例，并标注了每次引导的成功与失败。通过分析模型的早期解码动态，我们提取了特征，以理解引导效果如何在层和标记位置之间传播。最终，我们训练了一个梯度提升决策树分类器，能够在不进行完整生成的情况下预测引导的结果，从而优化引导强度的搜索。

papers.title: Dense Supervision, Sparse Updates: On the Sparsity and Geometry of On-Policy Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract On-policy distillation exhibits sparse parameter updates that are distributed across layers and favor FFN components, while maintaining geometric properties distinct from standard dense parameter rewriting.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct On-policy distillation (OPD) has recently become a prominent post-training recipe as it combines two desirable ingredients: on-policy student trajectories and dense teacher supervision, yet how this hybrid changes a model's parameters remains unclear. Across several language and vision-language model pairs and use cases, our analysis yields two main findings. On sparsity, OPD-style updates are small and coordinate-sparse. They are distributed across layers and are usually FFN-heavy. This sparse structure is operationally useful: training only the discovered subnetwork recovers nearly the same performance as full OPD. However, the sparsity-inducing SGD optimizer underperforms AdamW in our optimizer ablation, likely because dense teacher supervision preserves heterogeneous coordinate-wise gradient scales where AdamW's adaptive scaling remains useful. On geometry, the updates are numerically full-rank but spectrally concentrated; they lie mostly away from the principal singular subspaces of the source weights and fall disproportionately on coordinates where the source weights are close to zero. These findings suggest that dense teacher supervision does not turn OPD into ordinary dense parameter rewriting; instead, OPD retains important geometric signatures of on-policy post-training.
affiliations: National Key Laboratory for Novel Software Technology, Nanjing University
School of Artificial Intelligence, Nanjing University

ru.title: Редкие и геометрически структурированные обновления в дистилляции по текущей политике
ru.desc: В статье исследуется механизм обновления параметров при дистилляции по текущей политике (OPD) на примере языковых и мультимодальных моделей. Авторы обнаружили, что обновления параметров являются разреженными, сконцентрированы в компонентах FFN и распределены по слоям модели, однако их можно эффективно воспроизвести, обучая только найденную подсеть. Геометрический анализ показал, что хотя обновления имеют полный ранг, они спектрально сконцентрированы и сосредоточены на координатах, далеких от главных сингулярных подпространств исходных весов. Эти результаты демонстрируют, что плотный надзор учителя не превращает OPD в обычное плотное переписывание параметров, сохраняя важные геометрические свойства обучения по текущей политике.

en.title: Sparse Updates, Rich Learning: The Power of On-Policy Distillation
en.desc: This paper explores on-policy distillation (OPD), a method that combines student learning from current data and guidance from a teacher model. The study finds that OPD leads to sparse updates in model parameters, primarily affecting feedforward neural network (FFN) components while preserving unique geometric properties. The results indicate that training only a selected subnetwork can achieve performance similar to full OPD, although the sparse gradient descent optimizer used is less effective than AdamW. Overall, the research highlights that OPD maintains distinct characteristics compared to traditional dense parameter updates, emphasizing the importance of the training geometry.

zh.title: 政策蒸馏：稀疏更新与几何特性并存
zh.desc: 本文探讨了在政策蒸馏（On-policy Distillation, OPD）中，参数更新的稀疏性和几何特性。研究发现，OPD的更新主要集中在前馈神经网络（FFN）组件上，并且这些更新在各层之间分布不均。尽管使用稀疏结构的子网络训练可以达到与完整OPD相似的性能，但稠密教师监督的存在使得更新保持了重要的几何特征。总的来说，OPD在参数更新上与传统的稠密重写方式有所不同，保留了独特的几何特性。

papers.title: P3D-Bench: Benchmarking MLLMs for Parametric 3D Generation and Structural Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Parametric 3D generation benchmark evaluates models on geometric precision, semantic alignment, and assembly consistency through code-based modeling tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Multimodal large language models can write code to produce complex programs as well as use programs to do 3D modeling, which opens up a new avenue for 3D generation powered by their priors, world knowledge and reasoning. Yet existing benchmarks rarely evaluate 3D modeling through code. Such modeling demands more than runnable code: from a text or visual specification, a model must generate a parametric 3D program that is geometrically precise, semantically aligned and assembly-consistent. We introduce P3D-Bench, a benchmark for parametric 3D generation. Unlike a 3D mesh, a parametric 3D program exposes explicit dimensions, construction operations and part relations, revealing whether a model recovers a design's structure, not just its appearance. Under a unified protocol, P3D-Bench covers three task families (Text-to-3D, Image-to-3D and Assembly-3D) and scores each output for executability, geometric fidelity, topology, text-grounded constraints, multiview semantic alignment and part-level structure. We evaluate frontier MLLMs and text-only LLMs on 400 text cases, 400 image cases and 203 annotated assemblies, with domain-specific models as reference points. Our extensive evaluation yields three findings. First, assemblies are the hardest setting, where models still fail to compose multiple parts into a coherent structure. Second, models can often recover the global shape and semantic identity of the target object, yet fail to reproduce the precise parametric geometry specified by the input. Third, part-level modeling remains weak on assemblies, where models recover neither the geometry of each part nor the right number of parts. These results position P3D-Bench as a benchmark for evaluating precise parametric geometry and part-level structure in parametric 3D generation.

ru.title: Оценка точности параметрической 3D генерации через кодовое моделирование
ru.desc: В работе представлена P3D-Bench — бенчмарк для оценки моделей генерации параметрической 3D геометрии на основе текста, изображений и сборок. Многомодальные большие языковые модели способны писать код для создания сложных 3D программ, но существующие бенчмарки редко оценивают такие возможности систематически. P3D-Bench проверяет не только исполняемость кода, но и геометрическую точность, семантическое соответствие спецификации и консистентность сборки из частей. Авторы провели обширную оценку современных MLLM и LLM моделей на 400 текстовых примерах, 400 примерах с изображениями и 203 аннотированных сборках, выявив, что модели испытывают наибольшие сложности с точной параметрической геометрией и моделированием структуры из отдельных компонентов.

en.title: P3D-Bench: A New Standard for Evaluating 3D Generation Models
en.desc: This paper introduces P3D-Bench, a new benchmark for evaluating parametric 3D generation models. It focuses on assessing geometric precision, semantic alignment, and assembly consistency in 3D modeling tasks generated by large language models. The benchmark includes three task families: Text-to-3D, Image-to-3D, and Assembly-3D, and scores outputs based on various criteria such as executability and multiview semantic alignment. The findings reveal challenges in assembling parts into coherent structures and highlight the models' difficulties in achieving precise parametric geometry and part-level accuracy.

zh.title: 参数化3D生成的新基准测试
zh.desc: 本文介绍了P3D-Bench，这是一个用于评估参数化3D生成的基准测试。它通过代码建模任务来评估模型的几何精度、语义对齐和组装一致性。与传统的3D网格不同，参数化3D程序明确展示了尺寸、构造操作和部件关系，能够更好地反映设计的结构。我们的评估发现，现有模型在组装任务中表现最差，且在恢复目标对象的精确几何形状方面仍存在不足。

papers.title: μ_0: A Scalable 3D Interaction-Trace World Model
papers.abstract: Abstract A scalable world model called μ₀ uses 3D traces to predict smooth trajectories for key interaction points, enabling embodiment-agnostic robot learning without action labels.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct World models that capture how actions induce physical change enable scalable robot learning without reliance on embodiment-specific action labels. Pixel-space video models provide broad visual priors but expend model capacity on dense appearance reconstruction, while direct action models require embodiment-specific labels that hinder scalability. We present μ_0, a scalable world model based on 3D traces. Rather than predicting dense pixels or directly modeling actions, μ_0 forecasts smooth 3D trajectories for salient interaction points such as objects, tools, hands, and contact regions, yielding a compact, embodiment-agnostic motion interface. To enable training from diverse video sources, our TraceExtract system automatically extracts 3D supervision by selecting keypoints, constructing globally aligned traces, and associating motion segments with hierarchical language captions. This TraceExtract supervision pretrains μ_0 by combining a pretrained vision-language backbone with a modular trace expert, which represents each query via B-spline control points and predicts future traces. Experiments show that μ_0 outperforms baselines in both 2D and 3D trace prediction, including trace prediction models and tokenized VLM methods. Because μ_0 is frozen and reusable, it can be paired with action experts for downstream robot embodiments. Despite action-free pretraining, the resulting trace-conditioned policies achieve performance competitive with VLA models pretrained with action supervision, such as π_0. These results establish 3D traces as a scalable and transferable representation for cross-embodiment manipulation.

ru.title: Трёхмерные траектории как универсальное представление для кросс-воплощённого обучения роботов
ru.desc: В работе представлена модель μ₀, которая предсказывает гладкие трёхмерные траектории ключевых точек взаимодействия (объектов, инструментов, рук) вместо плотной реконструкции пикселей или прямого моделирования действий. Система TraceExtract автоматически извлекает трёхмерную разметку из разнородных видеоисточников, выделяя опорные точки и создавая глобально согласованные траектории с иерархическими текстовыми описаниями. Модель μ₀ обучается путём комбинирования предобученного видео-языкового энкодера с модульным экспертом по траекториям, представляющим кривые через управляющие точки B-сплайнов. Такой подход обеспечивает масштабируемое обучение роботов без привязки к конкретной морфологии и позволяет достичь производительности, сравнимой с моделями, обученными на разметках действий.

en.title: Scalable Robot Learning with 3D Trajectories
en.desc: The paper introduces μ₀, a scalable world model that predicts smooth 3D trajectories for key interaction points, facilitating robot learning without needing specific action labels. It utilizes a system called TraceExtract to automatically gather 3D supervision from diverse video sources, which helps in training the model effectively. Unlike traditional models that focus on pixel reconstruction or require specific action labels, μ₀ offers a compact, embodiment-agnostic interface for motion prediction. The results demonstrate that μ₀ not only outperforms existing models in trace prediction but also allows for competitive performance in robot manipulation tasks without action-based pretraining.

zh.title: 可扩展的3D轨迹模型μ₀：无动作标签的机器人学习新方法
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种可扩展的世界模型μ₀，它利用3D轨迹预测关键交互点的平滑轨迹，从而实现无动作标签的机器人学习。μ₀通过预测显著交互点（如物体、工具和手）的3D轨迹，提供了一种紧凑的、与具体形态无关的运动接口。该模型通过TraceExtract系统自动提取3D监督信息，结合预训练的视觉-语言模型和模块化轨迹专家进行训练。实验结果表明，μ₀在2D和3D轨迹预测方面的表现优于基线模型，证明了3D轨迹作为跨形态操作的可扩展和可转移表示的有效性。

papers.title: APT: Action Expert Pretraining Improves Instruction Generalization of Vision-Language-Action Policies
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers address poor generalization in Vision-Language-Action models by proposing APT, a two-stage training method that pretrains action experts using vision-action pairs before integrating language conditioning to improve out-of-distribution instruction performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models that couple pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) with continuous action experts have achieved strong manipulation performance, yet generalization to out-of-distribution (OOD) language instructions remains poor. A known challenge is the structural imbalance in VLA data, where language is far less diverse than visual and action content, making policies prone to visual shortcuts. While discrete-action methods mitigate this through vision-language co-training, continuous action experts lack such protection: they start from random initialization and learn entirely from imbalanced data, producing noisy gradients that corrupt the VLM and fail to exploit its language capability. We address this from a Bayesian perspective, factorizing the policy into a language-agnostic Vision-Action (VA) prior and a language-conditioned VLA likelihood, and propose APT, a two-stage training method emphasizing Action expert PreTraining. In Stage 1, the action expert is pretrained as a VA prior on vision-action pairs from a frozen VLM, bypassing the language imbalance. In Stage 2, language tokens are injected through a gated fusion mechanism that integrates VLM features while preserving the learned visuomotor prior. APT applies to mainstream VLA architectures, including the π and GR00T-style architectures. Comprehensive experiments validate that APT achieves consistent gains on unseen instructions and compositional tasks. Project Page: https://xukechun.github.io/papers/APT/

ru.title: Предварительное обучение экспертов действий для улучшения языковой генерализации в робототехнике
ru.desc: Исследователи решают проблему плохой генерализации в моделях Vision-Language-Action, предлагая APT — двухэтапный метод обучения, который предварительно обучает экспертов действий на парах vision-action перед интеграцией языкового кондиционирования. Основная идея заключается в факторизации политики с использованием байесовского подхода: разделении её на language-агностический Vision-Action приор и language-обусловленное VLA правдоподобие. На первом этапе эксперт действий предварительно обучается как VA приор на замороженной VLM, что позволяет избежать дисбаланса языковых данных. На втором этапе языковые токены интегрируются через gated fusion механизм, который сохраняет выученный visuomotor приор. Метод показывает стабильные улучшения на unseen инструкциях и композиционных задачах.

en.title: Enhancing VLA Models with APT for Better Generalization
en.desc: This paper introduces APT, a two-stage training method designed to enhance the generalization of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models. The first stage involves pretraining action experts using vision-action pairs, which helps to overcome the imbalance in language and visual data. In the second stage, language conditioning is integrated through a gated fusion mechanism, allowing the model to leverage its learned visuomotor skills while incorporating language. The results demonstrate that APT significantly improves performance on out-of-distribution language instructions and complex tasks.

zh.title: 提升视觉-语言-动作模型的泛化能力
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种名为APT的两阶段训练方法，以解决视觉-语言-动作模型在处理未见指令时的泛化能力不足问题。APT的第一阶段通过视觉-动作对对动作专家进行预训练，避免了语言数据的不平衡。第二阶段则通过门控融合机制将语言信息注入模型，同时保留已学习的视觉运动先验。实验结果表明，APT在未见指令和组合任务上均取得了显著的性能提升。

papers.title: Benchmarking AI Agents for Addressing Scientific Challenges Across Scales
papers.abstract: Abstract SciAgentArena presents a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating AI agents in real scientific research scenarios, revealing current limitations in novel insight generation and open-ended problem solving while identifying opportunities for improving agent reliability and autonomy.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct AI agents are increasingly being developed to accelerate scientific discovery, yet their practical capabilities in real research settings remain poorly understood. Existing benchmarks for AI agents rarely capture the complexity, heterogeneity, and extended reasoning required by scientific work, whereas benchmarks for scientific tasks often reduce research to static, direct problems and provide limited support for interactive evaluation. Here, we introduce SciAgentArena, a systematic benchmark for evaluating AI agents in real-world scientific research scenarios drawn from emerging needs across multiple domains. SciAgentArena comprises approximately 200 tasks with stepwise verification and an interactive, agent-agnostic environment for assessing diverse AI agents. Using this benchmark, we find that current agents can contribute effectively to well-specified data-analysis workflows, particularly when the task structure and evaluation criteria are clear. However, their performance remains uneven across scientific contexts: agents struggle to generate genuinely novel insights, sustain self-directed exploration, and formulate robust solutions for open-ended research questions. We further characterize common failure modes across agents and identify opportunities for improving their reliability, autonomy, and scientific reasoning. Together, SciAgentArena provides a practical framework for measuring progress in AI agents for science and for guiding the design of future agents capable of addressing complex scientific challenges. Full codes, tasks, and datasets can be accessed via this link: https://sciagentarena.github.io/.
affiliations: Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard

ru.title: Оценка способностей AI-агентов в сложных научных исследованиях
ru.desc: SciAgentArena — это комплексный бенчмарк для оценки AI-агентов в реальных научных исследованиях, содержащий около 200 задач с пошаговой верификацией. Исследование показывает, что современные агенты хорошо справляются с хорошо структурированными задачами анализа данных, но испытывают значительные трудности с генерацией новых научных идей и решением открытых исследовательских вопросов. Авторы выявили типичные ошибки в поведении агентов и определили направления для улучшения их надёжности, автономности и научного рассуждения. Бенчмарк предоставляет практическую основу для измерения прогресса AI-агентов в науке и разработки более совершенных систем.

en.title: Unlocking AI's Potential in Scientific Discovery
en.desc: SciAgentArena is a new benchmark designed to evaluate AI agents in real scientific research environments. It highlights the current limitations of these agents in generating novel insights and solving open-ended problems. The benchmark includes around 200 tasks that require stepwise verification and allows for interactive evaluation of various AI agents. The findings indicate that while agents perform well in structured data-analysis tasks, they struggle with self-directed exploration and formulating solutions for complex research questions, pointing to areas for improvement in their reliability and autonomy.

zh.title: 评估AI代理在科学研究中的新基准
zh.desc: SciAgentArena是一个全面的基准测试，用于评估AI代理在真实科学研究场景中的表现。该研究揭示了当前AI代理在生成新见解和解决开放性问题方面的局限性，同时指出了提高代理可靠性和自主性的机会。通过约200个任务和互动环境，SciAgentArena能够系统地评估不同的AI代理在科学研究中的能力。研究发现，尽管现有代理在明确的数据分析工作流程中表现良好，但在生成新见解和应对开放性研究问题时仍然存在困难。

papers.title: AlloSpatial: Agentic Harness Framework for Spatial Reasoning in Foundation Models
papers.abstract: Abstract AlloSpatial framework enhances spatial reasoning in foundation models by converting egocentric observations into structured allocentric representations and enabling reliable spatial cognition through cognitive mapping and tool-use reasoning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Multimodal Foundation Models (MFMs) have made substantial progress, yet remain fragile in spatial reasoning over the physical world. A key bottleneck lies in their inability to transform local egocentric observations into a global allocentric spatial representation. To address this, we propose AlloSpatial, an agentic framework for allocentric spatial cognition in foundation models. AlloSpatial introduces World2Mind, a plug-and-play cognitive mapping sandbox that converts egocentric observations into structured allocentric priors, including Allocentric-Spatial Trees and route maps that support querying object topology, geometric relations, passability, and trajectories. To utilize these priors reliably under noisy reconstruction and ambiguous visual evidence, AlloSpatial introduces a Spatial Reasoning Harness for tool-use judgment, modality-decoupled cue collection, and geometry-semantic arbitration. We further internalize this process in Qwen3-VL through cold-start reinforcement learning with a harness-gated trajectory-level reward. Experiments on VSI-Bench and MindCube show that AlloSpatial improves proprietary models by 5%-18% in a training-free setting, while ASTs alone support strong spatial reasoning even when visual inputs are removed. The trained AlloSpatial agents further outperform larger general-purpose models and competitive spatial baselines, suggesting that structured allocentric representations, active tool use, and verifiable reasoning offer a promising route toward spatially capable foundation models.
affiliations: Institute of Artificial Intelligence, Beihang University
University of Science and Technology Beijing

ru.title: От эгоцентрического взгляда к глобальному пониманию пространства
ru.desc: AlloSpatial — это фреймворк, который улучшает пространственное мышление в мультимодальных Foundation Models путём преобразования эгоцентрических наблюдений в структурированные алоцентрические представления. Система использует когнитивное картографирование (World2Mind) для создания древовидных структур и маршрутных карт, которые поддерживают запросы о топологии объектов и геометрических отношениях. Для надёжного использования этих представлений при шумных данных предлагается специальный инструмент Spatial Reasoning Harness, который помогает моделям принимать решения о применении инструментов и разрешать неоднозначности. Метод показывает улучшение на 5%-18% по сравнению с существующими моделями и демонстрирует, что структурированные пространственные представления помогают Foundation Models лучше рассуждать о физическом мире.

en.title: Transforming Observations for Superior Spatial Reasoning
en.desc: The AlloSpatial framework enhances the spatial reasoning capabilities of foundation models by transforming egocentric observations into structured allocentric representations. This is achieved through cognitive mapping techniques that create allocentric spatial trees and route maps, allowing the model to understand object relationships and spatial dynamics better. Additionally, the framework incorporates a Spatial Reasoning Harness to improve tool-use judgment and manage ambiguous visual inputs effectively. Experimental results demonstrate that AlloSpatial significantly boosts performance in spatial reasoning tasks, outperforming larger models and establishing a new standard for spatial cognition in machine learning.

zh.title: 增强空间推理的AlloSpatial框架
zh.desc: AlloSpatial框架通过将以自我为中心的观察转换为结构化的以他为中心的表示，增强了基础模型的空间推理能力。该框架引入了World2Mind，一个可插拔的认知映射沙箱，能够生成支持查询物体拓扑、几何关系和路径的以他为中心的先验知识。为了在噪声重建和模糊视觉证据下可靠地利用这些先验知识，AlloSpatial还引入了空间推理工具，帮助进行工具使用判断和几何语义仲裁。实验结果表明，AlloSpatial在空间推理方面显著优于现有模型，展示了结构化的以他为中心的表示和主动工具使用的潜力。

papers.title: Squeeze-Release: Iterative Pruning with Exact Structural Minimization
papers.abstract: Abstract Squeeze-Release compression method combines pruning with structural minimization to create significantly smaller neural networks while maintaining accuracy, extending to transformer architectures through CompensatedLayerNorm.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Unstructured pruning produces sparse weight tensors, but the standard implementation keeps tensor shapes unchanged so the deployed model is no smaller than before pruning. We present an exact structural rewrite, which we call minimization, that converts a masked network into a smaller dense network with the same forward function up to floating-point rounding. The Squeeze-Release cycle iterates pruning and minimization with an intermediate release step that re-enables the exact-zero positions inside the compacted tensors as small calibrated noise, turning otherwise wasted capacity back into trainable parameters. Successive cycles use that capacity to find structural redundancy a single pass cannot reach. We additionally introduce CompensatedLayerNorm, a function-preserving replacement for LayerNorm that extends minimization to channel reduction across LayerNorm-equipped residual streams. Squeeze-Release compresses the deployable network to 39x smaller than the unpruned model on a fully-connected model network and 14.8x smaller on modern CNN (ConvNeXt-Tiny), at comparable accuracy. In addition we prove that the rewrite can be extended to transformer architectures.
affiliations: Department of Information Technology, Uppsala University, Uppsala, Sweden
Science for Life Laboratory, Uppsala University, Uppsala, Sweden

ru.title: Сжатие нейросетей через итеративную pruning и структурную оптимизацию
ru.desc: В статье представляется метод Squeeze-Release, который комбинирует pruning (обрезку весов) со структурной минимизацией для создания значительно меньших нейронных сетей без потери точности. Авторы предлагают точное структурное преобразование, которое конвертирует сжатую сеть с масками в меньшую плотную сеть с идентичной функцией прямого прохода. Метод использует итеративные циклы pruning и минимизации с промежуточным этапом release, который переводит нулевые позиции в маленький калибруемый шум, превращая неиспользуемую емкость обратно в обучаемые параметры. Для трансформеров авторы разработали CompensatedLayerNorm - функционально эквивалентную замену LayerNorm, которая позволяет расширить минимизацию на редукцию каналов в residual streams.

en.title: Squeeze-Release: Shrinking Neural Networks Without Losing Accuracy
en.desc: The Squeeze-Release compression method enhances neural network efficiency by combining pruning and structural minimization, resulting in smaller models without sacrificing accuracy. It addresses the limitations of unstructured pruning by implementing a structural rewrite that transforms masked networks into smaller dense networks while preserving their functionality. The process involves iterating between pruning and minimization, with a release step that reactivates zeroed-out weights as small noise, effectively utilizing previously wasted capacity. Additionally, the introduction of CompensatedLayerNorm allows for further optimization in transformer architectures, achieving significant size reductions in deployable networks.

zh.title: Squeeze-Release：高效压缩神经网络的创新方法
zh.desc: Squeeze-Release压缩方法结合了剪枝和结构最小化，能够在保持准确性的同时显著缩小神经网络的规模。该方法通过CompensatedLayerNorm扩展到变换器架构。我们提出了一种精确的结构重写，称为最小化，将掩码网络转换为具有相同前向功能的更小的稠密网络。Squeeze-Release循环通过迭代剪枝和最小化，利用中间释放步骤将紧凑张量中的确切零位置重新启用为小的校准噪声，从而将浪费的容量转化为可训练参数。

papers.title: AdaSR: Adaptive Streaming Reasoning with Hierarchical Relative Policy Optimization
papers.abstract: Abstract An adaptive streaming reasoning framework enables models to dynamically allocate computation and reason during input streaming while maintaining task performance and reducing latency.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large reasoning models typically follow a read-then-think paradigm: they observe the complete input, reason over a static context, and then produce the answer. Yet many real-world scenarios are inherently dynamic, such as audio and video stream, where information arrives as a continuous stream and models must reason, update, and respond under partial observations. Recent streaming reasoning methods allow models to think while reading, but they largely rely on supervised imitation of pre-constructed trajectories, which limits their flexibility. In this paper, we propose AdaSR, an adaptive streaming reasoning framework that enables models to reason during input streaming and perform final deliberation once the stream is complete, learning when to think, and how much computation to allocate across different stages. To optimize this hierarchical reasoning process, we introduce Hierarchical Relative Policy Optimization (HRPO), which decomposes policy optimization into streaming reasoning and deep reasoning phases, providing more fine-grained advantage assignment instead of uniformly distributing a single sequence-level advantage over all tokens. HRPO integrates format, accuracy, and adaptive thinking rewards to enforce valid reasoning protocols, preserve final task performance, and encourage latency-aware computation allocation. Experiments show that AdaSR achieves a better balance among reasoning accuracy, computational efficiency, and streaming latency compared with supervised fine-tuning baseline. We release our code at https://github.com/EIT-NLP/StreamingLLM/tree/main/AdaSR.

ru.title: Думаю по мере поступления: адаптивные рассуждения в режиме реального времени
ru.desc: В статье представлена AdaSR — фреймворк адаптивного потокового рассуждения, который позволяет моделям динамически распределять вычисления во время обработки входящего потока данных. Авторы предлагают Hierarchical Relative Policy Optimization (HRPO) — метод оптимизации политики, разбивающий процесс рассуждения на этапы потокового и глубокого анализа для более точного назначения преимуществ. Фреймворк интегрирует награды за формат, точность и адаптивное мышление, чтобы сохранить корректность рассуждений и производительность модели при снижении латентности. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что AdaSR достигает лучшего баланса между точностью рассуждения, вычислительной эффективностью и задержкой обработки потока по сравнению с методами supervised fine-tuning.

en.title: Dynamic Reasoning for Real-Time Data Processing
en.desc: This paper introduces AdaSR, an adaptive streaming reasoning framework designed for machine learning models to process input streams dynamically. Unlike traditional models that analyze complete data before reasoning, AdaSR allows models to reason and update their understanding as data arrives, which is crucial for real-time applications like audio and video. The framework employs Hierarchical Relative Policy Optimization (HRPO) to optimize the reasoning process by separating it into streaming and deep reasoning phases, enhancing flexibility and efficiency. Experiments demonstrate that AdaSR improves reasoning accuracy and reduces latency compared to existing methods, making it a significant advancement in adaptive computation for streaming data.

zh.title: 自适应流式推理：动态计算与实时响应的结合
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种自适应流式推理框架AdaSR，旨在使模型在输入流中动态分配计算资源并进行推理，同时保持任务性能并减少延迟。传统的大型推理模型通常遵循先读取后思考的模式，而AdaSR允许模型在读取的同时进行推理，适应动态信息流的需求。我们引入了分层相对策略优化（HRPO），将策略优化分解为流式推理和深度推理阶段，从而实现更细粒度的优势分配。实验结果表明，AdaSR在推理准确性、计算效率和流式延迟之间取得了更好的平衡。

papers.title: Quickest Detection of Hallucination Onset: Delay Bounds and Learned CUSUM Statistics
papers.abstract: Abstract Token-level hallucination detection is reformulated as a quickest change detection problem, revealing fundamental limits on detection delay and demonstrating superior performance through causal recurrent modeling.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Token-level hallucination detectors are evaluated as classifiers, by AUC over all tokens, yet a streaming monitor is judged by its reaction time: the number of tokens that pass between the onset of a hallucination and the alarm. We formulate hallucination onset detection as a quickest change detection problem. A first-order Markov model of the latent faithful/hallucinated state, validated on RAGTruth, places the task inside classical change-point theory and yields Lorden's lower bound on detection delay: about 1.3 tokens at a false-alarm rate of 0.01. We then show that a causal recurrent labeler acts as a CUSUM with a learned increment; at a matched false-alarm rate it detects in 11-13 tokens, against 31 for a linear per-token baseline, and a controlled decomposition attributes most of this advantage to a better per-token score rather than to temporal accumulation. An information-rate optimality theorem of Donsker-Varadhan type explains the remaining order-of-magnitude gap: the learned score realizes only 1/4.5 of the divergence the features carry, a deficit that recalibration cannot remove, with the remainder a finite-horizon effect. Classification metrics conceal this delay structure; sequential analysis makes it measurable

ru.title: Обнаружение галлюцинаций в потоке через оптимальное изменение точек
ru.desc: В работе задача обнаружения галлюцинаций на уровне отдельных токенов переформулируется как проблема быстрого обнаружения изменений в статистических свойствах последовательности. Авторы применяют теорию изменения точек и показывают фундаментальное ограничение на задержку обнаружения - около 1.3 токенов при заданном уровне ложных срабатываний. Они демонстрируют, что причинная рекуррентная модель эквивалентна алгоритму CUSUM с обучаемым приращением и достигает задержки в 11-13 токенов, значительно превосходя линейный базовый метод. Анализ информационной скорости объясняет оставшийся разрыв между достигнутой производительностью и теоретическим оптимумом.

en.title: Detecting Hallucinations Faster with Change Detection
en.desc: This paper addresses the problem of detecting token-level hallucinations in machine learning models by framing it as a quickest change detection issue. It highlights the limitations of detection delay and introduces a causal recurrent model that outperforms traditional methods. The study uses a first-order Markov model to establish a theoretical lower bound on detection delay, demonstrating that the proposed method can significantly reduce the number of tokens needed for detection. The findings reveal that while classification metrics may overlook detection delays, sequential analysis provides a clearer measurement of performance.

zh.title: 最快变化检测：提升幻觉检测的效率
zh.desc: 本文将令牌级幻觉检测重新定义为最快变化检测问题，揭示了检测延迟的基本限制，并通过因果递归建模展示了优越的性能。研究表明，幻觉的开始检测可以通过经典的变化点理论来处理，并且在假警报率为0.01时，检测延迟的下限约为1.3个令牌。使用因果递归标签器的检测效果显著优于线性每令牌基线，且大部分优势归因于更好的每令牌评分。最后，信息速率最优性定理解释了剩余的差距，表明学习的评分只能实现特征所携带的发散的1/4.5。

papers.title: WaveDiT: Distribution-Aware Wavelet Flow Matching for Efficient 3D Brain MRI Synthesis
papers.abstract: Abstract WaveDiT generates full-resolution 3D brain MRIs using conditional flow matching in wavelet coefficient space with adaptive precision modeling, enabling efficient synthesis on standard hardware.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large and demographically balanced datasets are essential for reliable neuroimaging biomarkers. Full-resolution 3D brain MRI synthesis can support data augmentation in this setting, but existing approaches either incur prohibitive computational cost at volumetric scale or rely on lossy latent compression that may compromise anatomical detail. As a result, practical 3D generative augmentation often requires specialized compute infrastructure. We propose WaveDiT, a conditional flow matching framework operating in the coefficient space of a 3D Haar Discrete Wavelet Transform. The model combines factorized spatio-depth attention with band-wise heteroscedastic uncertainty modeling derived from higher-order wavelet statistics. Predicted log-variance is integrated directly into both the flow objective and conditioning pathway, enabling adaptive precision consistent with the heavy-tailed and input-dependent variance structure of anatomical detail. This formulation supports full-resolution 3D synthesis under practical memory and time constraints on a single modern GPU. Evaluation on a multi-site cohort demonstrates improved alignment between generated and real MRI distributions, together with enhanced downstream brain age prediction and region-level anatomical agreement relative to diffusion, latent, and wavelet-based baselines. Code is available at https://github.com/sisinflab/WaveDiT

ru.title: Полнораз решённый синтез МРI мозга через адаптивное flow matching в вейвлет-пространстве
ru.desc: WaveDiT - это метод условного flow matching для синтеза полноразрешённых трёхмерных МРI снимков мозга, работающий в пространстве коэффициентов вейвлет-преобразования. Модель использует факторизованное пространственно-глубинное внимание и адаптивное моделирование точности через гетероскедастическую неопределённость, вычисляемую из статистики вейвлет-коэффициентов. Предсказываемая дисперсия логарифма встраивается как в целевую функцию flow matching, так и в систему кондиционирования, обеспечивая синтез с высокой эффективностью на обычном GPU. Подход превосходит существующие диффузионные и латентные методы по качеству распределения сгенерированных изображений и их применимости для аугментации данных в нейровизуализации.

en.title: Efficient 3D Brain MRI Synthesis with WaveDiT
en.desc: WaveDiT is a novel framework that generates high-quality 3D brain MRIs using a technique called conditional flow matching in the wavelet coefficient space. It addresses the challenges of existing methods that either require expensive computational resources or lose important anatomical details due to compression. By utilizing a 3D Haar Discrete Wavelet Transform and incorporating adaptive precision modeling, WaveDiT efficiently synthesizes full-resolution images on standard hardware. The model has shown improved performance in aligning generated MRIs with real data and enhancing brain age predictions, making it a valuable tool for neuroimaging research.

zh.title: WaveDiT：高效生成3D脑部MRI的创新方法
zh.desc: WaveDiT是一种生成全分辨率3D脑部MRI的模型，采用条件流匹配技术在小波系数空间中进行自适应精度建模。这种方法能够在标准硬件上高效合成MRI图像，避免了传统方法在体积规模上的高计算成本。通过结合空间-深度注意力机制和基于高阶小波统计的异方差不确定性建模，WaveDiT能够在实际的内存和时间限制下实现全分辨率合成。评估结果显示，生成的MRI与真实MRI的分布更为一致，并且在脑龄预测和区域级解剖一致性方面优于现有的生成方法。

papers.title: ActiveMimic: Egocentric Video Pretraining with Active Perception
papers.abstract: Abstract ActiveMimic pretraining framework recovers camera and wrist trajectories from egocentric video to enable active perception learning that matches robot data performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Egocentric human video offers a scalable alternative to robot data for pretraining, yet models pretrained on such video consistently underperform those pretrained on robot data. We attribute this gap to a missing signal, the active perception behavior in egocentric videos, where humans continuously reposition their viewpoint during manipulation, inducing camera motion that standard pipelines treat as noise. To address this, we present ActiveMimic, a pretraining framework that recovers synchronized camera and wrist trajectories from a single body-worn RGB camera, models camera motion as a viewpoint action, and jointly learns active perception and manipulation from in-the-wild egocentric human video before adapting to a target robot. Empirically, real-world experiments across tasks with diverse active perception demands show that ActiveMimic consistently surpasses baselines pretrained on human video and matches state-of-the-art models pretrained on robot data. Further analysis provides evidence that active perception capability originates from egocentric human video pretraining rather than robot-specific fine-tuning, confirming active perception as the key to unlocking egocentric human video for robot pretraining.

ru.title: Активное восприятие робота через человеческое видео от первого лица
ru.desc: ActiveMimic — это фреймворк предварительного обучения, который восстанавливает траектории камеры и запястья робота из эгоцентрического видео человека, чтобы обучить робота активному восприятию окружающей среды. Ключевая идея состоит в том, что движения камеры в видео от первого лица, которые обычно игнорируются как шум, на самом деле содержат важную информацию об активном восприятии — умении перемещать точку зрения во время манипуляции объектами. Модель совместно обучается предсказывать как движения камеры (действие восприятия), так и движения робота из видео людей, а затем адаптируется к конкретному роботу. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что предварительное обучение на человеческом видео с ActiveMimic позволяет достичь производительности моделей, обученных на данных робота.

en.title: Unlocking Robot Learning with Active Perception from Human Videos
en.desc: The ActiveMimic pretraining framework enhances robot learning by recovering camera and wrist trajectories from egocentric videos, which are recordings from a person's perspective. Traditional models struggle with egocentric video data because they treat the camera motion as noise, missing the important active perception signals. ActiveMimic addresses this by modeling camera motion as a viewpoint action, allowing the robot to learn both active perception and manipulation from human video. Experiments show that robots pretrained with ActiveMimic outperform those trained on standard human video and match the performance of those trained on robot data, highlighting the importance of active perception in robot learning.

zh.title: 主动感知：解锁第一人称视频的关键
zh.desc: ActiveMimic是一种预训练框架，通过从第一人称视频中恢复相机和手腕轨迹，促进主动感知学习，以匹配机器人数据的性能。尽管第一人称人类视频为预训练提供了可扩展的替代方案，但基于此视频预训练的模型表现通常不如基于机器人数据的模型。我们认为这种差距源于缺失的信号，即在第一人称视频中，人类在操作过程中不断调整视角，导致相机运动被标准流程视为噪声。ActiveMimic通过从单个佩戴的RGB相机中恢复同步的相机和手腕轨迹，将相机运动建模为视角动作，并共同学习主动感知和操作，从而在适应目标机器人之前，利用真实世界的第一人称人类视频进行预训练。

papers.title: CARVE: Certified Affordable Repair of Vetoed Maneuvers via Envelopes for Interactive Driving
papers.abstract: Abstract Interactive driving scenarios reveal failures in autonomous vehicle rule systems where small adjustments could restore feasibility, leading to the introduction of CARVE, a certification framework that provides runtime proofs for multi-agent repairs without requiring predictions of other drivers' compliance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Interactive driving exposes a failure mode that is easy to miss in rule-aware autonomous-driving stacks: a hard-rule margin can be negative for an ego candidate even though a small lawful accommodation by a non-priority agent would restore feasibility. Existing rulebooks, shields, and reachability filters are strong at vetoing unsafe actions, while prediction-based planners model likely responses. Neither returns a runtime proof object that states which bounded multi-agent edit repairs the maneuver, who owns the edit, whether the request is right-of-way affordable, and what ego fallback remains if the request is not observed. We formulate this missing object as *interactive repair certification* and introduce *CARVE*, a prediction-free certificate layer over a finite lattice of ego-owned and agent-owned tactical operators. Agent-owned requests are admissible only inside \(B_j(s) = β(π_j)α_j^{\max}(s)\), a cooperation envelope that separates kinematic reachability from normative priority. The resulting certificate records the binding rule, repair category, repair set, responsibility-weighted cost split, and fallback. On 589 Lanelet2-geometry-grounded INTERACTION replay episodes, CARVE-Greedy accepts 98.64% of initially vetoed maneuvers and recovers 370/378 human-resolved false vetoes, while preserving 589/589 right-of-way respect, zero priority-agent false positives, and 400/400 negative-stress vetoes. We prove certificate soundness, structural right-of-way respect, exact finite-lattice minimality, fallback contingency, and blame-consistency conditions. CARVE does not predict or require another driver's compliance; it certifies whether a proposed interaction is bounded, attributable, and normatively admissible under declared assumptions.
affiliations: Department of Mechanical Engineering, McGill University, QC, H3A 2T7, Canada

ru.title: Безопасное вождение через сертифицированное кооперативное взаимодействие
ru.desc: В работе представлена CARVE — фреймворк сертификации для автономных транспортных средств, решающий проблему ложных отказов в системах планирования, которые не учитывают возможность небольших законных корректировок поведения других участников движения. Предложенный подход использует решётку тактических операторов и вводит концепцию интерактивной сертификации ремонта маневра, которая не требует предсказания действий других водителей. Каждый сертификат записывает применённое правило, категорию ремонта, множество возможных исправлений, распределение ответственности и стратегию отката, обеспечивая полную прослеживаемость. На наборе данных из 589 реальных сценариев движения CARVE достиг высокой точности, приняв 98,64% ранее отклонённых маневров при полном соблюдении приоритета и правил дорожного движения.

en.title: CARVE: Certifying Safe Interactions in Autonomous Driving
en.desc: The paper introduces CARVE, a certification framework designed to improve the decision-making of autonomous vehicles in interactive driving scenarios. It addresses the limitations of existing systems that rely on predictions of other drivers' behaviors, which can lead to unnecessary vetoes of safe maneuvers. CARVE provides runtime proofs for multi-agent repairs, ensuring that interactions between vehicles are feasible and compliant with traffic rules. The framework has been tested on numerous driving episodes, demonstrating high acceptance rates of previously vetoed maneuvers while maintaining safety and respect for right-of-way rules.

zh.title: CARVE：自动驾驶的交互修复认证框架
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了一种名为CARVE的认证框架，用于在多智能体环境中修复自动驾驶车辆的行为，而无需预测其他驾驶者的反应。CARVE能够提供运行时证明，确保在交互驾驶场景中，即使在规则系统出现失败的情况下，也能恢复可行性。该框架通过定义合作范围，记录绑定规则、修复类别和责任分配等信息，确保自动驾驶系统的安全性和合规性。实验结果显示，CARVE在处理被否决的驾驶行为时表现出色，能够有效恢复大部分人类解决的错误否决。
